event: edema of the right gluteal region
CT of the abdomen and the pelvis
inflammatory infiltration of the subcutaneous tissues of the hypogastrium and the penis
liquefaction and presence of gas in the subcutaneous tissues of the scrotum, the perineum, and the right gluteal region
excision of the penile and scrotal skin
able to breathe on his own with oxygen on low flow
diuresis stimulated using a small dose of furosemide
culture of the pus material showed Escherichia coli and Pseudomonas aeruginosa
antibiotic therapy modified to include cephazolin
transferred to the Department of Plastic Surgery
free-skin grafts applied to the debrided areas
follow-up in the urological and surgical clinic
application of the free-skin graft to the right gluteal area
